description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the CountAdditionalEscapeChars function in src/objects/js-regexp.cc. The function uses a 32-bit signed int variable 'escapes' to count the additional characters required when escaping a RegExp source string. For each U+2028 (LINE SEPARATOR) or U+2029 (PARAGRAPH SEPARATOR) character in the source, 'escapes' is incremented by 5, because each such character is replaced by a 6-character escape sequence ('\u2028' or '\u2029'). When the RegExp source string contains approximately 430 million or more U+2028/U+2029 characters, the accumulated value of 'escapes' exceeds INT_MAX (2,147,483,647) and wraps around to a large negative value due to signed 32-bit integer overflow. In the PoC, a string of ~457.9 million U+2028 characters causes escapes to overflow to -2005397586 (= 457913942 * 5 mod 2^32 interpreted as signed), triggering the debug assertion DCHECK_GE(escapes, 0) at line 234. In a release build, execution continues to EscapeRegExpSource, which computes 'length = source->length() + additional_escape_chars' with the overflowed (negative) value, resulting in an undersized buffer allocation. WriteEscapedRegExpSource then writes beyond the allocated buffer, causing a heap buffer overflow.

fixes.evidence_text: [https://crrev.com/c/6611425](<https://crrev.com/c/6611425>) approved for merges, please feel free to merge to 13.8, 13.7, 13.6 at your earliest convenience; there is no rush on this and can wait until after the Monday MUC holiday
fixes.description: Local patch exported from https://crrev.com/c/6611425 for reproducible fixed-image builds.
